original: Voice of a 3,000 year old Egyptian priest recreated by scientists 3D printing his vocal tract published in "Scientific Reports"
Chinese researchers report they have cloned two monkeys, using the same technology as Dolly the Sheep
Twelve camels disqualified from the King Abdulaziz Camel beauty contest, Saudi Arabia after their owners used botox on their lips

US Navy bathyscaphe "Trieste", crewed by Swiss oceanographer Jacques Piccard and USN Lieutenant Don Walsh, becomes first manned vessel to reach bottom of Pacific Ocean (10,916 m) in the Mariana Trench; craft was designed by Auguste Piccard, father of the pilot, in 1953 for the French Navy
Final communication between Earth and NASA space probe Pioneer 10 then 7.6 billion miles (12.23 billion kilometers) from Earth
Clyde Tombaugh photographs dwarf planet Pluto
Mauna Loa volcano in Hawaii begins an eruption that lasts 300 days
-80°F (-62°C) in Prospect Creek Camp, Alaska (US record)

Wham-O Company acquires rights to the "Pluto Platter" plastic flying disc toy from inventor Walter Frederick Morrison; they rename it "Frisbee" in 1958 as a nod to the Frisbie Pie Company, whose empty tins were tossed by Yale University students (including Morrison) in the 1930s
Helgafell volcano on island of Heimaey Iceland erupts for 1st time in 7,000 yrs

Elva Zona Heaster is found dead in Greenbrier County, West Virginia. Resulting murder trial of her husband perhaps only case in US history where the alleged testimony of a ghost helped secure a conviction.
Elizabeth Blackwellbecomes 1st woman in the US to earn a medical degree graduating from New York's Geneva Medical College
Sweden becomes the first nation in the world to ban aerosol sprays, due to their harmful effect on the earth's ozone layer.
Urbe Blanca (cow) produces record 110 kg of milk, Cuba (approx date)
Temperature falls from 44°F (7°C) to -56°F (49°C) night of 23-24, Browning, Montana
Storm Éowyn hits Ireland and the UK with record-high winds of 114 mph (184 km/h), leaving hundreds of thousands without electricity in Northern Ireland and one person dead
Jerrold R. Zacharias, American physicist who designed the first atomic clock, born in Jacksonville, Florida (d. 1986)
Tatiana Proskouriakoff, Russian-American Mayan archaeologist who helped decipher Maya hieroglyphs, born in Tomsk, Russia (d. 1985)
First radio rescue at sea using the CQD distress code by the British Royal Mail steamship Republic off Nantucket Island
Golden State guardKlay Thompsonscores the most NBA points in a quarter (37) and most 3-pointers in a quarter (9) in Warrior's 126-101 win over Sacramento Kings
LeBron Jamesbecomes the seventh and youngest player (33 years, 24 days) to reach the 30,000 NBA point milestone during a 114-102 loss to the San Antonio Spurs
NHL New York IslandersMike Bossybecomes 1st in NHL to score 50 goals in 50 games

Michael Jordanscores in double figures for 800th consecutive game in a 100-98 OT win over New Jersey; adds 40 more double-figure scoring games to his NBA record before retiring for the second time (1998)
Detroit Red Wings score 15 goals against New York Rangers with an NHL record 37 points; also record consecutive goals & most lopsided game, 15-0
Buffalo wide receiver Gabriel Davis scores an NFL playoff record 4 TDs in the Bills' epic 42-36 overtime loss to the Kansas City Chiefs in their divisional-round playoff at Arrowhead Stadium
Detroit Red Wings scores NHL record 8 goals in 1 period
Greatest unpaced 1-hr bicycle distance, F Moser (Italy), 51.15 km
Patrick de Gayardon, French skydiver and skysurfing pioneer, born in Oullins, France (d. 1998)
Pakistani cricket batsmanHanif Mohammadscores record 337 runs in 970 minutes in drawn 1st Test v West Indies at Bridgetown, longest innings in Test history
UCLA loses to Notre Dame, UCLA then wins next 88 games in a row
The first version of the Java programming language released
Category 5 blizzard delivers record 3ft of snow to the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast US, killing 55 and incurring estimated $500m - $3bn economic losses
Norwegian coastal town of Ålesund is devastated by fire, leaving 10,000 people homeless and one person dead. KaiserWilhelm IIfunds the rebuilding of the town in Jugendstil style.
TV series "The A-Team" with George Peppard and Mr T premieres on NBC
Miniseries "Roots" premieres on ABC
8 museum workers from Egyptian Museum, Cairo referred for prosecution for reattachingTutankhamun's beard with inappropriate glue
William Morgan, American physical education instructor and inventor of "mintonette" (now known as volleyball), born in Lockport, New York (d. 1942)
Robert Parris "Bob" Moses, American civil rights activist (Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee (SNCC)), and educator (The Algebra Project), born in Harlem, New York (d. 2021)
Ed Roberts, American activist for disability rights, born in San Mateo, California (d. 1995)
Daniel Hale Williamsforms the Provident Hospital in Chicago, the first non-segregated hospital in the US
Duke Ellingtonplays at Carnegie Hall in New York City for the first time

Inaugural class of the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame inductees:Chuck Berry;James Brown;Ray Charles;Sam Cooke;Fats Domino; The Everly Brothers;Buddy Holly;Jerry Lee Lewis;Elvis Presley;Little Richard;Robert Johnson;Jimmie Rodgers; Jimmy Yancey; Alan Freed; John Hammond; and Sam Phillips
Bulgarian Saint John of Rila [Saint Ivan] performs the miracle of the two pies - uses two pies to feed the poor
In a coronation ceremony, Zhu Yuanzhang, aged 40, ascends to the throne of China as theHongwuEmperor, initiating Ming Dynasty rule over China that lasts for three centuries

War elephant corps of the Southern Han defeated at Shao by crossbow fire from Song Dynasty troops forcing the Southern Han state to submit to the Song Dynasty. First regular war elephant corps in the Chinese army.
Tunisia becomes the first Arab nation to outlaw slavery in decree issued by Ahmed I Bey
Groundbreaking for NACA (now NASA) Lewis Research Center
US launches second generation weather satellite, ITOS 1
Australia's 1st amateur radio satellite (Oscar 5) launched (California)
Ian Redpath hits his only 2 sixes in Cricket Tests, v WI Adelaide
Chita Rivera, American Tony Award-winning dancer, singer, and actress (West Side Story; Bye Bye Birdie; Sweet Charity; Chicago), born in Washington, D.C. (d. 2024)[1][2]
Robert Cabana, American astronaut (STS 41, 53, 68, sk:88), born in Minneapolis, Minnesota
Omar Henry, South African cricketer (1st colored player for South Africa 1992), born in Stellenbosch, South Africa
Anita Pointer, American pop and R&B singer (The Pointer Sisters - "He's So Shy"; "I'm So Excited"), born in Oakland, California (d. 2022)
Richard Dean Anderson, American actor (MacGyver, Emerald Pt NAS), born in Minneapolis, Minnesota
John Luther Adams, American composer ("Become Ocean" Pulitzer Prize for Music 2014), born in Meridian, Mississippi[1]
Robin Zander, American rock vocalist and guitarist (Cheap Trick - "Dream Police"; "I Want You To Want Me"), born in Beloit, Wisconsin
Richard Finch, American disco and rock bassist, songwriter and producer (KC and the Sunshine Band - "Shake Your Booty"; "I'm Your Boogie Man"), born in Indianapolis, Indiana
Margarete Schütte-Lihotsky, Austria's first female architect who designed the first fitted kitchen, born in Vienna, Austria (d. 2000)[1]
Walter M. Miller Jr., American sci-fi author (Hugo; View from Stars), born in New Smyrna Beach, Florida (d. 1996)
